ROBERTSON, COLIN CHARLES, M.F.—Probationary forest offr., O.F.S., Feb., 1903; studied forestry in Europe and at Yale Univ., 1905-07; ast. conservator of forests, O.F.S., 1907; dist. forest offr., and research offr., Pretoria, 1912; professional ast., 1917; on active service with S.A.H.A., Apr., 1917 to July, 1919; ast. conservator, forests, Eastern conservancy, 1919; Natal and Midland conservancies, 1920; resumed duties of prof. ast., Pretoria, Oct., 1920; conservator, forests, Oct., 1920.
ROBERTSON, GEORGE WATSON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Govt. pathologist, Cape Town and med. offr. in ch., govt. vaccine statn., Cape Town, 1921.

ROBERTSON, HON. GIDEON BROCKE.—B. 1874; ed. pub. and high schls., Welland, Ontario; called to the senate, Canada, 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, 1918; cabinet min. without portfolio; min. of labour in national govt., 7th Nov., 1918 to 29th Dec., 1921.

ROBERTSON, J. P.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; headmaster, govt. boys' schl., Accra, G. Coast, 11th Apr., 1908; inspr. of schls., 1st Jan., 1911; provincial inspr. of schls., 1st Jan., 1915; lieut., G. Coast volunteers.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1875; ed. Glenalmond, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; formerly house surgeon and physician, Swansea hosp. and Victoria Park hosp. for chest diseases; civil surg., S. African Field Force, 1901-2 (medal and three clasps); ast. comsrr. and gen. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1905; dist. comsrr. and gen. med. offr., Caicos Is., 1905; gen. med. offr., Grand Turk, 1909.
ROBERTSON, WALTER BANNERMAN.—B. 1854; ed. dist. schla., Marshfield, P. E. Island, high sheriff of Queen's Co., 1896 to 1902; collr. of cust. for the port of Charlottetown, Apr., 1902.

ROBERTSON, W. C. F., C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1886 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr. of schls., G. Coast, 1898; director of educ., 1899; ast. comptroller of cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ast. sec. for native affairs, 14th June to 19th Nov., 1905; prov. comsrr., 20th Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, 18th May, 1907; ast. col. sec., 4th July to 25th Sept., 1908; chief ast. col. sec., 22nd Jan., 1909; ast. col. sec., 1909; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; ast. col. sec., Sept., 1910, 11th May to 15th June, 1911, 14th July, 1911 to 5th Jan., 1912, 30th Oct. to 26th Dec., 1912, and 7th Mar. to 21st Aug., 1913; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April-May, 1913; ast. gov., 1st May to 27th Aug., 1914; dep. gov., 28th Aug. to 6th Sept., 1914; col. sec., Gibraltar, 12th Feb., 1915; lieut.-gov. and chief sec. to govt., Malta, May, 1917; offr., admstg. govt., Malta, 12th June, 1920 to 2nd July, 1920; govt.'s dep., 1st Oct., 1920 to 13th Oct., 1920; lieut. gov. by comsrr. under new constitution, 14th Apr., 1921; offr., admstg. govt., 16th May to 9th Sept., 1921 and from 23rd May to 17th Aug., 1923; govt.'s dep., 17th-24th Feb., 1923.

ROBIN, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.B. (1900).—B. 1861; ed. privately and at Otago high schl.: N. Zealand volr. offr.; comd. squadr., N. Zealand mtd. rifles, Queen Victoria's diamond jub., 1897; comd. 1st N. Zealand contngt., S. Africa, 1899-1901; comdt., Otago mil. div., 1902-07 (brev. col.); chief of gen. staff and 1st mil. mem. of coun. of defence, 1907-11 (col.); adjt. and qtrm.-gen., 1911-12; represented N. Zealand on Imperial gen. staff War Off., 1912-13; comdt. and G.O.C., N. Zealand mil. forces, 1914-19; maj.-gen., 1916.

ROBINSON, ALFRED FRANCIS.—Ed. privately and Durham Schl.; served European War, 1914-17; 6th K.A.R., E. Africa campaign, 1917-18; Uganda treas., 1911-13 (resigned); cust. dept., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919.

ROBINSON, AUBREY CHARLES.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; commr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compd. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (published deferred during the war); regnr.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in July and Dec., 1918, and from 25th Mar., 1919; regnr.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. regnr., supt. ct. and marshal, June to Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, Sept., 1923.

ROBINSON, FRANKLIN.—B. 1878; M.A., Durham Univ.; barrister, Inner Temple, 1915; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1902; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, Apr., 1903; demarcation offr., Malacca, 4th Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1904; mag., June, 1904; passed cadet, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, Dec., 1906; ag. dep. regnr. of sup. ct., sheriff, mag., commr. of ct. of requesta, regnr. of marriages, and chmn. of bd. of licensing justices, Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1908; 3rd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Nihong Tebal, 5th Apr., 1912; ditto, Butterworth, 22nd Aug., 1913; ag. regnr., sup. ct., dist. judge and mag., Malacca, 26th Sept., 1913; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Aug., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Krian, 9th Aug., 1915; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, 28th Dec., 1915; services lent to Kelantan govt., 14th Dec., 1917; dist. offr., Temerloh, 16th Jan., 1919; ag. astt. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. II., F.M.S., Jan., 1920; ag. regnr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1920; ag. legal adviser and judge, Kedah, Oct., 1920; judge, 1st divn. under new courts enactment, continued to act as legal adviser in addn., 8th Sept., 1921; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor, Penang, 1st Mar., 1922.

ROBINSON, J. ALEX, M.B.E. (1919).—J.P., and notary pub. for the col. (Newfildld.); ed. Vict. Coll., Jersey, and New Kingwood, Bath; prin. of Carbonate gram. schl., 1883-92; elec. for Bonavista, 1897; col. sec. of Newfildld. and mem. of exec. coun., 1897; accepted leadership of legis. coun., Dec., 1897; May, 1898, retd. to H.A. for dist. of Trinity; mem. treasy. bd., 1898; M.L.C., 1910; postmr.-gen., 1916.

ROBINSON, LIEUT.-COL. SIR THOMAS BILBE, K.O.M.G. (1913), KT. BACHE (1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commnr. for Franco-British exhib.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comte., for settm. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

ROBINSON, VICTOR GEORGE MELLOR, J.P. (Natal).—Clt., Eshowe magistracy, 22nd Aug., 1892; gov't.'s off., 1st Nov., 1892; treasy., 27th Oct., 1893; res. commr.'s off., 1st Oct., 1896; 1st clk., 27th Nov., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., chief mag. and civ. commr.'s off., prov. of Zululand, master, chief mag.'s ct., and register, chief mag.'s and high cts., 30th Dec., 1897; sec. to chief mag. and civ. commr., 1st Oct., 1900; 1st cls. clk., 1st Nov., 1900; mag., 1st Jan., 1904; transf. to treasy., 9th Jan., 1906; under-treas., 1st May, 1906; ag. under sec. for finance, U. of S. Africa, 1910; mem., pub. serv. comm., 1st Aug., 1912; chmn., pub. serv. comm., Aug., 1921.

ROBISON, CHAS. CARR.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and St. Paul's, Stony Stratford; served with several banking companies, etc.; attached to treasury, Sarawak, 1901; supt. of cust., 1902; postmr.-gen., shipping-mast. and supt. of cust., 1904.

ROCHE, HON. WILLIAM JAMES, M.D., LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Lucan, Ontario, and high schl., London, Ontario, Trinity med. coll., Toronto; grad. M.D., with 1st cl. hon., Western Univ., London, Ontario, 1883; 1st grad. in medicine of that Univ.; LL.D., 1911; removed 1883 to Minnedosa, Man., where he is a practising physician; territorial rep. for Manitoba med. coun., 1886-1901; has held highest offices in I.O.O.F., Manitoba; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1896; re-elec. g.e. 1900, 1904, 1908 and 1911; conservative whip for the West, 1901-1911; mem. of the privy coun. for Canada, and sec. of state in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; min. of the Interior, 1912; chmn., civ. serv. comm., 1917.

ROCK, JOHN CHRISTOPHER WILBERFORCE.—B. 1882; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. astt. to govt. agt., S. Prov., June, 1906; Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1908; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1912; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1913; ditto, Kalutara, Aug., 1920; dep. commr. of excise, May, 1921.

RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1906; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementer clk., col. audit dept., Feb., 1914; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Kurunegala Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1913; office astt. to govt. agt., W. Prov., May, 1916; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1916; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1917; office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ditto, Matara, May, 1922.

RODWELL, SIR CECIL HUNTER, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high commr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov., Fiji, and high commr., W. Pacific, 8th Apr., 1918; assumed duty, 10th Oct., 1918.

ROGERS, ARTHUR WILLIAM, D.Sc., F.R.S.—B. 1872; ed. Clifton Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; Harkness scholar, Camb.; geologist, geological comm., Cape of Good Hope, 1896; asst. dir., geological surv., Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; dir. geological survey of Union of S. Africa, 1917.
ROGERS, RICHARD EDWARD ELMORE.—Govt. printer and comptroller of stationery, S. Australia, 1st June, 1910.
ROGERS, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1864; ent. civ. serv., S. Australia, 9th Mar., 1882; 2nd clk., audit dept., 1st Feb., 1912; audr.-gen., S. Aust., 1923.

ROGERS, WALTER JOHN LANCASHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1889; Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Kandy Kachchheri, Jan., 1913; ag. additional office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Jan., 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, May, 1914; addtl. office asst. Anuradhapura, June, 1914; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, June, 1914; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matale, Sept., 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Avisawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923.

ROLSTON, GEORGE MICHELL.—M.D., C.M., Univ. of Manitoba, Canada, 1895; L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.R.C.S., Edin.; L.R.F.P. and S., Glasgow, 1898; mem., Coll. of Phys. and Surgeons, 1903; F.R.I., pub. health, London; late civil surg., attached to R.A.M.C during S. African war, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal, 2 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); appdtd. to Leeward Is. med. serv., 1903; med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, and ag. med. offr. of pub. instit., 1903; dist. med. offr. of health, Vancouver, B. Columbia, 1908; med. offr., dist. 8, Nevis, 1914; ag. mag., Nevis, May to June, 1915; med. offr., dist. 6, Nevis, Sept., 1915.
ROOKE, C. E.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., 1914.

ROOS, J. DE V., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1869; advoo. of sup. ct., Cape Colony; ditto, S.A.R.; attorney, etc., of sup. ct. of S.A.R.; sworn translator in English, Dutch and Portuguese, sup. ct., Transvaal; Cape parly. service, 1893-7; ag. sec. of law dept., Transvaal, 1st Dec., 1908; ditto and ag. dir., prisons, Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1909; sec. to law dept., 1st May, 1909; re-admitted advoo., sup. ct., Transvaal, 26th June, 1909; dir., prisons, 1st July, 1909; sec. for just. and dir. prisons, Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; ag. contr. and audr.-gen., 1st Aug., 1918; contr. and audr.-gen., 9th Dec., 1918; nom., American rly. acctng. offr.' assn.; mem., joint comte. of the universities for professional exams.; mem., société de comptabilité de France.

ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and City and Guilds Engnr. Coll., S. Kensington; held various engnr. appts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1908; asst. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag.dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1922.

ROPER, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1891; entd. C.A. office, 1907; seconded to Falkland Is., Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to admstrn. and clk. to couns., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; ag. local auditor, Nov., 1914; returned to C.A. office, 1916; served European war (M.G.C., France), 1916-1919; 1st cls. clk., Nyasaland, 15th Jan., 1920; ag. ch. clk., July to Dec., 1921; editor, Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Nov., 1922.

ROSE, HON. HUGH EDWARD.—B. 1869; ed. at Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto (B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Faaken, Cowan, Chadwick and Roe; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, 4th Dec., 1916; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.

ROSEBY, THOMAS ERNEST.—B. 1868; ed. at Sydney Univ.; M.A.; honourman in French and German; senr. clk., Royal Mint, Sydney, 1885-1896; called to the bar, New S. Wales, 1896; also to high ct., of Australia; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1912; crown proct., N.S. Wales assizes, 1902-3; Brit. judge of joint ct. and judicial commr. for the New Hebrides, Nov., 1908; thanked by S. of S. for memoranda on land suits, 1910; co-author of the joint ct. civ., criminal and land registration procedure codes; puisne judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Feb., 1917; chmn. of govt. wages commn., 1919; chmn. of comte. to advise on treatment of former enemy aliens, 1919; chmn., govt. scholarship grants comm., and chmn., rly. enquiry comm., 1923.

ROSS, ALEXANDER HOWARD.—B. 1880; ed. Epsom Coll. and on H.M.S. "Worcester"; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1905; ag. inspr., P.S., 1908 and 1909; ag. dist. commr. and travelling commr., 1908 and 1909 in Ashanti and Northern Territories; asst. dist. commr., Ashanti, 1910; ag. prov. commr., 1912 and 1913; ag. dist. commr., 1913; dist. commr., 1914; ag. prov. commr., Mar. to Oct., 1916; passed in criminal law, Tropical African Service Course: dist. commr., 1917-18; ag. prov. commr., Mar. to Sept., 1919; ag. sec., native affrs., May to June, 1919; prov. commr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920.

ROSS, CHARLES RICHARD.—Clk., P.O. savings bank, Cape, 1890; agric. dept., 1894; clk. and acctnt., office of conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, Cape, 1895; dist. forest offr., George, Cape, 1897; Kokstad, Trankei, 1901; asst. conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1907; transf. to King William's Town, 1910; conservator of forests, Eastern conservancy, Cape, 1st Jan., 1912; ditto, Western conservancy, Oct., 1916.

ROSS, EDWARD ANDREW.—B. 1895; ed. Leigh Gram. Schl. and Manchester Univ.; on active serv., 1914-19; awarded M.C.; relinquished commn., 1921 with rank of capt.; cadet, F.M.S., 1920; ag. asst. contr. of lab., May, 1921; superv. offr., cls. V., May, 1923.

ROSS, GEORGE ARCHIBALD PARK, M.D., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.); diploma, trop. diseases, Liverpool.—B. 1879; S. African War, Lovat's Scouts, 1900-02; Anglo-Portuguese bndry. comm., Zambesia, 1904-5; native rebellion, Natal, 1906; trop. diseases investigtn., 1906-10; gov't. pathologist, Natal, 1911; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, statnd. in Natal, 1918; major, S. African med. corps, in ch. sleeping sickness bureau, Durban, and sanitation offr., Natal, 1914-19.

ROSS, J. G. H.—Asst. dist. commr., K.A.P., Sept., 1919; ag. junr. asst. sec., Kenya, 10th Nov., 1919 to 26th Oct., 1919; ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., 12th Dec., 1919 to 14th Mar., 1920; editor, offl. Gazette, 6th Feb., 1920 to 31st Mar., 1920.

ROSS, JAMES PAULL.—B. 1881; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A.; writer to the signet; capt., 5th Batt. the Royal Scot. (Q.E.R.) T.; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 13th Aug., 1913; ag. dist. commr., 1914, 1915, 1916 and 1917; junr. asst. col. sec., 8th Jan., 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1918; dist. commr., 9th Apr., 1919; ag. pol. mag., Coomassie, 12th Apr., 1919 to 6th Feb., 1920; ch. census offr. in addn., 19

ROSS, OSWALD F.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; capt., N. Staffs. Regt., European War; served Dardanelles expdn., 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916; passed A.T.S. course, 1920; ast. dist. comsnr. Gold Coast, 1920.
ROSS, WILLIAM ERNEST.—B. 1870; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 20th Aug., 1890; ch. clk., surg.-gen.'s office, and sec. to bd. of health and quarantine authority, 1st Jan., 1912.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Demy of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.O.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthorp. societies of Berlin and Florence, and of anthorp. inst., Lond.; late ch. protector of aborigines, Queensland; and Royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of Indians, and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana, 8th Dec., 1906.

ROTIMI, J. F. AMOSU.—Ed. Baptist elementary schl. and academy, Lagos; marine clk., Calabar, Oct., 1895; ast. regisr. of ct., Calabar, June, 1896; dep. regisr. of ct., Jan., 1899; trav. supervisor of native ots., S. Nigeria Prot., Sept., 1901, to Apr., 1907; ditto, E. Prov., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1907; has recd. thanks of govt. for services in connection with reorganization of native courts, S. Nigeria, 1902-3.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; dep. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1899; ag. Crown pros., Feb., 1900; Crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAM.—B. 1856; ed. St. Mary's Hall, Southsea, and Cadet school; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; ast. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; ag. marine supt., Dec., 1903, to June, 1904; comdr., H.M.C.S.Y., "Ivy," S. Nigeria, June, 1906; sen. marine offr., R. Prov., S. Nigeria, May, 1907.

ROWATT, HUGH HOWARD.—B. 1861; ed., Ottawa Coll. Inst., and St. Catherine's Affiliated Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1887, as correspondent; ch. clk., mining lands br., Mar., 1907; contrlr., mining lands, 1912; supt., 1920.

ROWE, MAJOR R. H., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Wellington Coll. and Royal Milly. Acad., Woolwich; 2nd lieut., Royal Arty., 1902; served Bermuda and Jamaica, 1902-6; served with native infantry under Sir H. Trenchard in S. Nigeria, 1907-10; Northern Hinterland exped., 1907-8; Niger Cross River exped., 1908-9; operations against Abini, 1909, and against Munchis, 1910; lent to survey of Nigeria, 1910; dep. dir., 1911; Nigeria Dahomey bndry. comsnr., 1912; Cameroons, 1914-16; ch. of admstv. staff, with major-gen. Sir C. Dobell, France, 1916-18; commanding howitzer batty. and brig. major, 11th corps, Heavy Arty., Russia (Murman coast), 1918; three times men in despa.; surv.-gen., G. Coast, 1920.

ROWELL, THOMAS HERBERT.—Ck., loco. dept., I.M.R., Pretoria, Oct., 1900; acctg. brch., ch. loco. supt.'s office, Jan., 1903; acctg. offr., ch. mech. engnr.'s dept., July, 1908; inspr., audit dept., S.A.R. and Harbrs. Off., 1910; inspr., ast. contr. and audr. gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, May, 1911; inspr., (ryls. and hbrs.) control and audit dept., 1st July, 1916; senr. inspr., 1st Oct., 1922.

ROWLEY, FORTESCUE WILLIAM.—B. 1874; ed. Canterbury Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. dept. of labr., 1893; dep. chief inspr. of factories, 1907; chief clk. and dep. chief inspr., 1911; sec. of labr., chief inspr. of factories, regtr. of industrial unions, and supt. of workers' dwellings, 1914.

RUDDICK, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1862; dairy expert; ast. to dairy comsnr., Canada, 1891; prin. dairy soh., Kingston, Ont., 1896; dairy comsnr., New Zealand, 1898; ast. to comsnr. of agr., Can., 1900; dairy comsnr., Can., 1906; dairy and cold storage comsnr., Can., 1906.

RUGGLES, NEPEAN CLARKE, K.C., LL.B.—Barrister of sup. ct., Nova Scotia; Bachelor of Laws at Dalhousie Coll., Halifax, Canada, with honrs., 1891; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, with highest honours, 1891; K.C., Nova Scotia, Jan., 1911, served with the Canadian forces during S. African War; now on the reserve of officers. Canadian militia; public prosecutor at Barberton, Transvaal, 1902; ast. res. mag., 1903 to 1907; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Oct., 1909 to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., Roseau and regisr., sup. ct. in addition on various occasions; took census of Dominica, 1911; mem., leg. coun., Dominica; mag., Grenada, 1915 to 1920; ohmn., several comuns., while in Grenada; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just. for short period; 1st ols. stip. mag., B. Guiana, Apr., 1921.

RUNDLE, GEN. SIR HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1856; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1898; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. R.A., 1876; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Zulu war, 1879; Boer war, 1881; Egyptian war, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-5; Soudan Frontier field force, 1885-7; Soudan Frontier, 1889; Dongola expdn. force, 1896; adjt.-gen., Egyptian army; Khartoum expdn., 1898; comdg. S.E. Dist., 1898-9; dep. adjt.-gen. to the forces, 1899-1900; in comd. of div., Aldershott, 1900; lieut.-gen. on staff in comd. of 8th Div., S. African field force, 1900-1902; lieut.-gen. of Nthn. Comd., 1903-7; col.-comdt., R.A., 1907; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Malta, 1909-1915.

RUSHMER, JAMES.—B. 1876; entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896; promoted, after exam., under order-in-council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., 18th Feb., 1911; ast. sec. to colonial blue-books comtee., 1917-1918; seconded for serv. at overseas settlement office, Apr., 1920.

RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—Ck., comsnr. of rlys. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsnr. of rlys., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901; sec., W., Australian London Agency, 3rd Nov., 1919.

RUSHTON, HERNERT HARVEY.—B.1876; acct., electric light dept., Gibraltar, 1897; ast. sec. and ast. acct., munici. authority, 1902; ch. acct. and ast. acc., 1903; sec. and finan. offr., 1908 (also man., pub. electricity and water supplies, mem., comtee. on exchange and currency); ast. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., E. Africa Prot., 1915; lieut., E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., 1916; ag. dep. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. ch. acct. to Dec., 1919 (also govt. rep., Nairobi munic. coun., 1918, mem., gen. manager's advisory comtee., 1919); dep. treas., Kenya Colony, 1920 and 1921; ag. treas., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. couns. (also ohmn., currency and other comtees.); col. treas., Fiji, 1922 (with charge of group of dept., cust., port and marine, transport, posts, telegraphs and telephones, govt. stores, and returned soldiers' dairy sttlmt.); mem., exec. and leg. couns.; commr. of currency, mem., educ., mining and other bds.

RUSSELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ANDREW HAMILTON.—K.C.B. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1868; held comm., as lieut. for 5 yrs. in Imperial Army; vol. offr., New Zealand, 1900; hon. A.D.C. to H.E. the Gov. of N.Z., 1911; brig.-gen., 1914; temp. major-gen., 1915; major-gen. in command of N.Z. Div., 1916; foreign decoration, Order of Danilo (Montenegro), 1917.

RUSSELL, HON. BENJAMIN, K.C., M.A., D.C.L.—Ed. at Halifax gram. schl. and Mount Allison Univ.; called to the bar, 1872; for some years law adviser to legis. coun., Nova Scotia; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1896; judge of supreme et., Nova Scotia, 1904.

RUSSELL, THOMAS BROWNLEE.—B. 1873; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford, M.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1897; office astt. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Mar., 1899; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1899; dist. judge, Tangalla, Aug., 1900; office astt. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Nov., 1901; Uva Prov., Nov., 1902; dist. judge, Batticaloa, July, 1903; office astt. to govt. agent, N. Prov., Nov., 1903; pol. mag., Jaffra, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. judge, Jaffra, Mar., 1904; pol. mag., Kandy, July, 1905; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1915; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Aug., 1915; acted on two occasions in 1915 and 1916 as addtl. agt. gov., E. Prov. in addition to his own duties; commnr. of requests, Colombo, Mar., 1918; dist. judge, Galle, Nov., 1920.

RUSSELLI, WILLIAM ALISON.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., LL.B., honours, 1897; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1900; equity bar, 1900-6; apptd. Crown advoc., pub. proscr., admstr.-gen., prin. regiar. of docmts., regiar. of companies, Uganda Prot., May, 1906; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Jan., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Aug., 1909, to Mar., 1910; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Mar. to May, 1910; ag. chief sec. to govt., June, 1910 to May, 1911; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., June, 1911 to June, 1912; King's advoc., Cyprus, July, 1912; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1912; joint commnr. under Reprint of Statutes Law, 1913; edition pubd., Feb., 1914.

RUSSELL, WILLIAM EAST.—Joined Fiji police, 27th April, 1891; inspr. of imigrts., 1897; J.P., 1901; ag. commnr., Colo N. and E., and stip. mag., Ra., 1902; ag. stip. mag., Ba., 1904; ag. stip. mag., Lautoka, 1905; ag. commnr., Colo N. and E., July, 1905, to Jan., 1907; astt. commnr. and stip. mag., Colo N. and E., 1908; commnr. and stip. mag., Colo E., June, 1910; Colo N., Nov., 1910; 1st grade dist. commnr., Apr., 1915.

RUTHERFORD, GEORGE JAMES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. Lond.—Ed. at Epsom Coll. and Middlesex Hosp.; astt. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; on spec. serv. in Lagos during the Sarki expdn., 1897-98; sen. med. offr., G. Coast, 1907; sen. med. officer, S. Nigeria, 1907; prov. med. offr., Ashanti, 1910; astt. prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr. gen. of hospitals, Ceylon, 1911; Ceylon deleg. at "All India" sanitary confee., 1911-12; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Ceylon, Oct. to Dec., 1912, Feb. to Dec., 1913, and from June to Aug., 1915; prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Aug., 1915; M.L.C.

RUTHERFORD, CAPT. PETER WILLIAM, M.C. (1916), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; 2nd lieut., W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1915; seconded, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; retld. from army, 1919; astt. dist. commnr., G. Coast, 26th Sept., 1921.
RUXTON, CAPT. F. H.—Gazetted Worcestershire Regt., 1896; seconded, Royal Niger Constab., 1898-1899; served in S. Africa, 1900; prov. admintr., N. Nigeria, 1901; let dls. res., Int Oct., 1908.

RYAN, HUGH KAYE, M.A., L.L.M., etc. (Cantab.).—Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple (1905; mem., Western Circuit; special pleader; author of several legal works; mag., B.E. Africa, 1913; joined Inns of Court O.T.C., 1914; depot adjutant; selected by milly. authorities for secret work, 1917; seconded for serv., Royal Engineers, 1918; joined directorate, I.W. and D. War Office; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mily. divn.); astt. atty.-gen. Jamaica, 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1922; P.B.C., Cyprus, 1923.

RYCROFT, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), C.B. (1910).—B. 1861; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined 71st H.L.I., 1879; served in Nile expdn. (including desert march to Metembeh), 1884-85 (medal and clasp and bronze star); Egyptian Army, 1886-87; action of Sarras (despa., 4th cls. Medjidie); promoted into 7th Dragoon Guards, 1888; staff coll., 1891-92; D.A.A.G., York, 1896-96; promoted into 11th Hussars, 1896; commanded 11th Hussars, 1904-8; staff, S. Africa, 1911-12; A.Q.M.G., Southern Command, 1913-14; service, N.W. Frontier of India, 1897-98 (medal and clasp); D.A.A.G., Cairo, 1898-99; served in S. Africa on staff, 1899-1900 (despa., medal and 2 clasps); Somaliland, 1902-3 (despa., medal with clasp); European War, 1914-18 (despa., 7 times, prom. maj.-gen., Knight of Grace, St. John of Jerusalem, Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur, Orders of White Eagle of Serbia, Redeemer of Greece, Star of Roumania, Greek Order of Merit 2nd cls.); retd. pay, 1921; gov., N. Borneo, 1922.
SADLER, C.—Astt. gen. manager, govt. rly., Trinidad, 1st Dec., 1914.

SAINTSBURY, JOHN SETMOUR.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in Evidence, Procedure and Criminal Law, bar exama., 1904; certif. of honour from council of legal educ. at Bar Final, 1905, and a prize of £50., from the Inn; passed civ. ser. test exam., Barbados, 1882; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., 1883; in P.O., 1884; 7th clk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clk., audit off., Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inland rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," July to Nov., 1906; ag. pol. mag., dist. "A," June to Nov., 1911, and from Apr., 1912 to June, 1913; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 30th June to 31st Oct., 1913; pol. mag. and judge of petty debt ct., dist. F., Sept., 1915; pol. mag., Dist. "E," 1919.
SALMON, T. G. A.—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.

SALMOND, HON. SIR JOHN WILLIAM, KT. BACH.(1918), M.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1862; ed. at Univ. of New Zealand and Univ. of London; parly. draftsman, N. Zealand, 1907-1909; solr.-gen., 1910-20; judge, sup. ct., 1920; N.Z. rep. on Br. delegation to Disarmament Confce., Washington, 1921.
SAMBIDGE, GERALD CHURCHILL.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham; served Nigeria Regt., 27th Mar., 1915; instr. in arts and crafts, N. Provs., Nigeria, 1st Dec., 1920.

SAMPSON, HON. VICTOR, K.C.—Served for 10 years in Cape civil serv.; passed B.A. and LL.B. exams., and obtained first Chancellor's medal, Cape Univ.; called to the bar, 1881; holder of special retainer to De Beers, 1898, and later, director; returned to Cape parlmt., 1898; attorney-gen., 1904; ag. puisne judge, Eastern dist., 1911-12; puisne judge, Eastern Dist., 15th July, 1915.

SAMUEL, RT. HON. SIR HERBERT LOUIS, P.C. (1908), G.B.E. (1920), M.A.—B. 1870; ed. University Coll. Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P., Cleveland Divn., N. Riding, Yorks., 1902-18; parly. under sec., home dept., 1905-9; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster (with a seat in the cabinet), 1909-10 and 1915-16; P.M.G., 1910-14 and 1916-16; pres. of the L.G.B., 1914-15; sec. of state for home affairs, 1916; chmn. of the select comte. of house of commons on national expenditure, 1917-18; British sp. commr. to Belgium, 1919; grand offr. of the Order of Leopold of Belgium, 1919; high commr., Palestine, 1920.

SAMUEL, SILVERIO I.—Ed. at St. Gregory's Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; messr. and copyt., educ. off., Oct., 1897; elk. to inspr. of schl., Mar., 1899; sec. to comte. for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; elk. and interpreter to trav. commr., Ilseha interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. elk., govr.'s off., Aug., 1904.
SANDESON, G. M.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., med. off., Nyassaland, 1910; temp. major, Nyassaland Field Force, Aug., 1914; sanitation off., Nov., 1921.

SANDESON, JAMES.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; elk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also elk. to quarantine bd. in 1880; solr., atty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. for Barbados to quarantine confce. at Demerara in 1888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbados rly. co. in 1891; commr. of probates, 1904; J.P., 1904.
SANDFORD, G. R.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; priv. sec. to govr., 1918; junr. asst. sec. and elk. of couns., Sept., 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1923.
SANDS, SIR JAMES PATRICK, KT. BACH.(1917).—B. 1859; mem. H. of A., Bahamas, 1889-1916, and leader for the govt., 1909-1916; M.E.C. since 1901; pres. of leg. coun., 1916.
SANDS, WILLIAM H.—B. 1869 pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1889; Out Island commr. (4th div.) and teacher, 1917.

SANDYS, MELVILL KEVENS TRELAWNY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; attd., Ratnapura Kach., Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, July, 1923.

SANGER-DAVIES, ARTHUR ELPHINSTONE.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll., Coopers Hill and Oxford (Diploma of Forestry, 1907); asst. conservator, forests, grade II., F.M.S. forest dept., 1907; grade I., 1912; dep. conservator, forests, 1919.

SARAVANAMUTTU, P.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Colombo, Kach., Aug., 1919; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Kalutara, Feb., 1920; attd. to Matale Kach., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, June, 1922; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923.
SARGENT, R. G.—Port. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S. S., Sept., 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1900; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apr., 1902; ag. sheriff, deputy-registr., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1906; asst. registr., sup. ct., Malacca, May, 1907; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1908; ag. registr., July, 1909; ag. dist. judge, May, 1915; ag. censor, Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. offl. assignee, Penang, in addition, May, 1916; registr. and sheriff, sup. ct., Penang, also asst. registr. of cos., Penang, Jan., 1917; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES JAMES, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., May, 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Mar., 1897; dist. offr., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., and aspt. of Ind. immigr., Penang, and munic. commr. for Penang, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1899; also ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Sept., 1898; 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, Aug., 1898; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1900; ag. offl. assignee, S. S., Sept. to Dec., 1900, Jan., 1901, to Feb., 1906, and Feb., 1906; sheriff, dep. registr., supreme court, and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munic. commr. for Sing., Oct., 1900, and Sept., 1903; also ag. prot. of Chin., Sing., Apr., 1904; also asst. supt. of Indian imgrts.; asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, May, 1904; ag. sec. for Chin. affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1907; ag. commr., et. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. judge, Apr., 1908; sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1910; ag. offl. assignee, Mar., 1914; registr. of cos. and offl. assignee, Jan., 1916; pres., rent assessment bd., Sept., 1917.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAUT, K.C.M.G., 1906, C.M.G., 1896.—B. 1857; held various appts. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (despa.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatonga-land and Zululand, 1889 (despa.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1883-96 (despa.), when he was apptd. spec. commr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. commr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag., and eiv. comsnr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Comm., 1902, and commr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, JOHN C.—B. 1862; ed. pub. schls. and High Schl., Halifax, N.S.; Acadia Univ., Wolfville, N.S.; ent. pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1882; transferred to finance dept., Ottawa, 1st July, 1887; ast. dep. min. of finance, 1st Jan., 1917; dep. min. of finance, 1st Apr., 1920.

SAUNDERS, REGINALD G.—Ed. at Tonbridge and Cheltenham Coll.; ag. 3rd ast. P.M.G., Ceylon, 13th Jan., 1898; ag. ast. collr. of cust., Trincomalee, Sept., 1898; office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and ast. collr. of cust., 1st June, 1901; pol. mag., Matara, 22nd Apr., 1903; extra ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1st Mar., 1904; ag. ast. govt. agt., Manar, 2nd May, 1904; comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Chilaw, 12th Feb., 1906; ag. ast. govt. agt. and dist. judge, 11th Apr., 1906; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Jan., 1909; ag. pol. mag. Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1909, and dist. judge, May, 1909; office ast., Colombo Kachcheri, Nov., 1911; lieut., Ceylon Light Infantry (reserve); on military duty as ast. censor, Dec., 1914; censor, May, 1915; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1915; ag. ast. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, May, 1920; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Sept., 1920; pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1923.

SAVAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; ast. of registration, G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; ast. of mails, ditto, 1st Jan., 1915; ag. ast. of mails, Penang, Jan., 1915; ag. senr. ast. of mails, Feb., 1917; ast. of mails, Penang, but continued ag. senr. ast. of mails, July, 1917; ast. grade II., Jan., 1919; ag. ast. gen. money order branch and savings dept., May, 1919 and Nov., 1920; ast. grade I., but cont. to act as above, Jan., 1920; ast. postal telegraph, Malacca, Sept., 1920; ag. ast. gen. money order branch and savings bank dept., 27th Nov., 1920; ag. ast. P.M.G., Singapore, 1st Apr., 1921; ag. acctnt., G.P.O., Singapore, 26th July, 1921 to 1st Nov., 1921; ag. ast. P.M.G., 25th July, 1922.

SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—Pol. probationer, S. Stltmtns., Nov., 1904; ast. ast. of pol., Jan., 1908; passed final exam. in Punjaubi, Feb., 1910; ag. ast. of pol., Aug., 1910 and Feb., 1914; ag. ast. of pol., Dec. to Feb., 1914; ag. ast. of pol., Malacca, Sept., 1916; ast. ast. of pol., Penang, Oct., 1917; ag. dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1919; dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1920; ag. ast. pol. and ch. pol. offr., Singapore, May, 1921; dep. comsnr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1922.
SAWREY-COOKSON, SYDNEY SPENCER, B.A.—B. 1876; ed. Uppingham and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1903; judicial comsnr., Brit. N. Borneo, 1910-20; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1920.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., 14th July, 1905; paymr. lieut.-comdr., 19th Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter; lent to C.O. and apptd. ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 15th Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. ast. col. sec., G. Coast, 16th Apr., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., 16th May, 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, and ag. trav. comsnr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., 30th Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part).

SAYER, GEOFFREY ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (foundationer) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner in classics and Fitzgerald exhibitioner; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, 24th Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. ast. dist. offr. (South), 1912 to 1913; ag. ast. supt. of police, 1913 to 1914; ag. dep. offr. recvr., 1914 to 1915; ag. chief ast. sec. for Chinese affairs 1914 and 1915, in addition to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addition to other duties, 1915; attached to crown solr.'s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1915; ag. chief ast. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1916; on milly. serv., 1915-19; dep. regis. and appraiser, Hong Kong, 1920; head of sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921.

SAYERS, ELDBED FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1st Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, FREDERICK RICHARDSON.—M.D., D.P.H., Dublin Univ.; ed. Rathmines Schl., Dublin, The Meath Hosp., Dublin, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; res. med. offr., Monkstown Hosp., Dublin, 1909-12; med. offr., S. Settmts., 23rd Mar., 1912; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, 27th Apr., 1912; med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, 2nd June, 1912; port health offr. and health offr., rural areas, Penang, cor., Penang, and med. offr. in charge of leper asylum, Pulau Jerejak, Penang, 3rd Sept., 1912; ag. ast. port health offr., Singapore, 10th Sept., 1912; resumed duties in Penang, 21st Oct., 1912; ast. censor, Penang, in addition, Sept., 1915, to Apr., 1916; ag. sen. health offr., S. Settmts., in addition, 1st Mar., 1917; ag. ast. health offr., rural areas, Singapore, 20th Mar., 1919; ag. chief health offr., S. Stltmtns., in addition, 4th Apr., 1919; lecturer in hygiene, King Edward VII. medical schle, Singapore, 10th June, 1919; ag. ch. health offr., Singapore, Oct., 1920; ag. senr. health offr., Penang, Jan., 1921.
SAYERS, G. F.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ast. sec., Tanganyika territory, 21st July, 1920.
SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parl. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916; now col. sec.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; ast. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. coman., Canada, 1915; ast. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-stabmt., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; contra and audit office, July, 1893; ast. examnr. accts., Jan., 1894; examr., May, 1898; ch. examr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec. educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, 17th Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; ast. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, 12th Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; ag. 2nd ditto, 1915; passed law exam., 1916; seconded to H. Kong and Shanghai Bank, 1918; ag. 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1918 to 1919, ag. dep. regar., and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919; ag. 2nd ast. to ditto, 1919, 1921 and 1923; ag. ch. ast., Aug., 1920 to Nov., 1921; dist. offr., South in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923.

SCHOMBURGK, OTTO HEINRICH.—Ck., engrn.-in-chief's office, S. Aust., Jan., 1874; elk., ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; elk., regar.-gen. of deeds office, July, 1880; elk., sheriff's office, Nov., 1882; dep. sheriff, 1891; also returning offr. W. Adelaide, Jan., 1893, to June, 1902; sheriff, July, 1903; major, comdg. field batt. of artillery (ret.).

SCHRADER, LOUIS WILLIAM CONRAD.—B. 1873; ed. Highgate School and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, April, 1897; pol. mag., Galagedara, July, 1899; off. ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1899; N. C. Prov., Mar., 1901; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1901; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1903; ast. coll. of cust., Trincomalee, Dec., 1904; dist. judge, Tangalla, Feb., 1905; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, April, 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1912; dist. judge, Matare, May, 1913; oomsnr. of requests, Colombo, Oct., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Kalutara, June, 1914; ditto, Negombo, Oct., 1915; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1915; ag. govt. ag., E. Prov., Sept., 1918; ditto, Prov. of Uva, June to Sept., 1919; govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1921; ditto, E. Prov., Oct., 1922; regar. gen., Mar., 1923; govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1923.

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regar., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigrn. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; librn., Malta public library, July, 1923.

SCIORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyceum Univ., Malta; in service of Eastern and S. African telegraph companies, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1903; ast. res., 1904; 3rd cls. res., 29th Apr., 1907; 2nd cls. res., 15th June, 1910; senr. res., 12th June, 1921.

SONCE, HERBERT W.—Ed. at Hurst Coll., Sussex; Gram. Schl., Bristol; Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. math. tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; called to the bar, Inner Temple, July, 1903; ast. master, Faversham Gram. Schl.; Godolphin Schl., Hammersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schl., Hackney, 1887 to 1893; ast. mr., Queen's Schl., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; ast. inspr. of schl., July, 1895; ag. inspr., May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., 1900; July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; inspr. of schl. and offl. visitor of Onderneeming Indust. Schl., Apr., 1903; dir. of primary educn., Sept., 1914; mem. bd. of agric. for educnt. matters; examr. for prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTLAND, DOUGLAS WILLIAM, F.S.I., N.D.A., N.D.D., H.A.A.C. Dip.—Ed. at Mussorie Schl., India and Alleynes Schl., Stone, Staffs.; Agric. Coll., Newport, Salop, 1904-1907; Salop County Scholar, 1906-1907; Profsnl. Assoc. of Survrs. Inst., 1909; agric. instr., Tyrone (Board of Agric. Ireland), 1907-08; lectr. at Harper-Adams agric. coll., 1908-11; ast. agric. dept., Sierra Leone, 1911-1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions; dir. of agric., Sierra Leone, 3rd Dec., 1915.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELISABETH.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, HON. DAVID LYNCH, K.C.—B. 1845; ed., Gram. Schl., Brampton, Ont.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1870; K.C., 1885; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1894, of sup. ct. of Alberta, 1907; ch. just., appellate divn., sup. ct., with style of ch. just. of Alberta, 15th Sept., 1921.

SCOTT, DUNCAN CAMPBELL, F.R.S.C.—B. 1862; apptd. to Indian commr.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. elk., dept. of Indian affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; 1st cl., 1889; ch. elkship., 1893; ch. acctnr. and supt. of Indian educn., 1908; deputy supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, 1913; hon. sec., Royal Soc. of Canada.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; M.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., London; Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; prosecutor, 1894; ast. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1896; ast. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willencl Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII. mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge hosp., Aldershot, 1916.
SCOTT, HERBERT SEPTIMUS.—B. 1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educnt. dept., Transvaal, 1911.

SCOTT, JOHN, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admng. the govt., Nov., 1903; off. ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1906; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910; ditto, Hambantota, May, 1911; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addtl. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1914; attached to censor's off., Apr., 1915; censor, Sept., 1915; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, 23rd Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to cls. IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindinga, Apr., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. comnrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, July, 1906, to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindinga, Aug., 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1907; ag. comnrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Oct., 1908; in charge of stllmt., Feb. to May, 1909, and from Feb. to July, 1910; assumed duties as collr. of land rev., Penang, July, 1910; ag. senr. dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1911; promoted to cls. ii., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1915; ag. temp. 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1915; censor, Penang, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge (civil), May, 1916; ag. res. and pres., munic. comnrrs., Malacca, Mar., 1920; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Jan., 1921.
SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1919; ment. in despatches, 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919.

SCOTT, THOMAS.—Clk., aud. office, Transvaal, 18th Feb., 1901; inspr., 1st Jan., 1904; prin. clk., revenue div., treasy., 1st Oct., 1904; rev. inspr., 1st Mar., 1905; prin. clk., audit office, 1st Dec., 1905; chief inspr. of expenditure audit, control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1913; ag. asst. contr. and audr.-gen., Feb. to Aug., 1921 and May to Oct., 1922.
SCOTT, WILLIAM DUNCAN.—B. 1861; comnrr. for Canada to the Paris Exhib., 1900; supt. of immigr., 1903-1919; asst. dep. min. of immigr. and colonization, 1919.
SCOTT, WM. ERNEST.—Ed. at Bradford and Giggleswick gram. schls., England; dep. min. of agric. and supt. of farmers' institutions, B. Columbia, 1908.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROOKE.—B. 1876; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Hertfd Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., 14th Aug., 1903; served in the Army in England and France from 8th Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH.—B. 1886; clk. to res. dep. comnrr., New Hebrides, 1902-1906; inspr. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. comnrr., 1912, and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, 1916.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1883; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg-gen.'s dept., Br., Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treasy. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comnrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasy., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-recevr., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1901.

SEARCY, ARTHUR.—Boarding offr., cust., S. Aust., 1873; clk., cust., 1874; clk., audit offr., 1875; clk., cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measure of ships, cust., 1889; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comnrr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; clk. asst. and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; dep. comnrr. of taxes and stamps, Jan., 1896, to Mar., 1897; comnrr., ditto, Mar. to Oct., 1897; dep. comnrr., ditto, Oct., 1897, to Feb., 1911; also pres., marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosene, 1902; comnrr. of trade marks and regir. of copyrights, 1904-7; also controller, outer harbour, Port Adelaide, Nov., 1907; also controller, ocean steamers wharf, Apr., 1909; also chief inspr. under Inflammable Oils Act, June, 1909; also supt. of life-saving service, Aug., 1909; also controller of harbours, July, 1911; chmn. of harbs. bd. commrs. since 1914.
SEARLE, HON. SIR MALCOLM WILLIAM, Kt. BACH. (1923), K.C.—Puissant judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), June, 1910; judge-pres., June, 1922.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cl. hist. tripos, pt. I., 1914 and pt. II., 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; asst. prin., C.O., 1922.

SEDWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed.; at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S. Stllmtns. civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supermny. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. treas. and comnrr. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macolesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv. with Cheshire Regt. from 20th Nov., 1914 to 23rd Jan., 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher divn. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air min'y., 28th July, 1919; res. clk., air min'y., 31st Oct., 1921; seconded for serv. in C.O. as asst. prin., 9th Jan., 1922.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. BR. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (desps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French gov.) for saving life, 1891; parly. under-secr. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comte., to inquire into organisation of crownagents'office, 1908; under-secr. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint parly. sec. to ministry of munition, 1918; under-secr. of state for air, Jan., 1919.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 12th Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., 1st Jan., 1916; asst. censor, 1914; censor, 18th Apr., 1918.
SEHULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; asst. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, 1st Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., 1st Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., 1st June, 1917.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws comm., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regar.'s office and clk. pub. market, 1892; asst. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treasy., 1898; sec. bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admistr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbrmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treas., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1911, and from Apr. to June, 1918.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1898; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., registr.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELANDER, JOHN KINAR, M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; rly. surveys and constrn., Cape Colony, 1896-1908; Nigerian rly., 1908-1920; exec. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria; served in Boer War and European War; temp. major, R.E.; ment. in desps.; dir., pub. wks., Tanganyika Territory, 1920.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (crest, 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1906), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-86; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-96; succeeded his father, 1896; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comte., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comte., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high comanr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELDON, A. A.—Asst. dist. comanr., R.A.P., Dec., 1913.

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Stltmsts., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postmr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909; offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. collr. of land rev. and regar. of deeds and regar. of Muhammadan marriages, Penang, 27th Mar., 1912; asst. censor in addtn. to other duties, 1st Jan., 1915; chmn., santi. bd., Kinta, 7th Aug., 1919; offr., registr. of titles, Perak, ag. supt., convict estab., and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., in addn., 5th Jan., 1920; ag. P.M.G., cls. I.A., S. Stltmsts., 1st Oct., 1920; ag. collr., land rev., and registr. of deeds and bills of sale, Singapore, in addn., cls. I.B., 14th Feb., 1921; ag. collr.-gen., income tax and collr.-gen., war tax, S. Stltmsts., cls. I.B., 1st Apr., 1921; ag. P.M.G., S. Stltmsts., cls. I.A., 1st Nov., 1921.

SELLS, MARTIN PERRONET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. asst. loco. supt., Lanes and Yorks. Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt. light rlys., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in desps.); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rlys., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., Tanganyika Terry. rlys., 1920; ch. mech. engrnr., Gold Coast rlys., 1922.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regar., Leeward Is., and regar. and prov. marshal, admistr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., regar. of titles, regar. of friendly societies, and regar.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1900; apptd. regar. and prov.-marshal, admistr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regar. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.
SIMPLE, J. M., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., L.M. (T.C.D.).—Med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922.
SENARATNE, CORNELIUS.—B. 1870; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; extra office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1921; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1922.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B'caio Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1922.

SENNITT, ORDIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Stltmsts., 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; asst. censor, 1916; asst. regar., impts. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., 28th Nov., 1921.
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief accts., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.
SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909.

SETH, G. G., B.A., Barrister-at-Law (Certif. of Hon.), 1910.—Calcutta; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1901; ag. aheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1908; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regar., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regar., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907; asst. to atty.-gen., 15th Dec., 1910; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn (cert. of hon.), 1910; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 6th Dec., 1912; collr., war tax, Singapore, 26th Mar., 1917; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; offr. asst. to atty.-gen., Singapore, Nov., 1918; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor in addition, May, 1919; has held numerous acting appointments; solr.-gen., S. Stltmsts., Mar., 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., civil and criminal assizes, Malacca, Apr., June and Oct., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. and offl. mem., select comtee. of leg. coun. on the constitution of the leg. coun. of the S. Stltmsts., 28th Oct., 1920 to 14th May, 1921; rep. of ocls. and prots. at Empire patent confce., London, June, 1922.

SEVERN, Sir Claud, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. clk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Stiltns., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; asst. sec. to res. gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high commr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov., S. Stiltns., May, 1907; sec. to high commr., Malaya States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admtd. govt., 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912, 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913, 6th Nov. to 8th Dec., 1916, 10th Nov. to 16th Dec., 1917, 12th Sept., 1918 to 29th Sept., 1919, 8th Apr. to 11th May, 1921 and from 18th June to Oct., 1922.

SEVIGNY, Hon. Albert, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; unsuccessful candidate, legis. assem., Quebec, bye-elec., 1907; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 3rd Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, Alfred Wallace.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; asst. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921.

SEYMOUR-HALL, Charles Cyril William, F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.C.I.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1919; capt. H.M.A.Z. 31, 1919; demob., 1919; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. rec., Zanzibar, 1923.

SHANKLAND, Walter Charles.—Clerical asst., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 18th Oct., 1896; engnr.'s asst., P.W.D., 5th Oct., 1887; 3rd clk., acctn.'s branch, P.W.D., 23rd May, 1889; 4th clk. clk., ditto, 1st Apr., 1892; 3rd cls. offr. and clk. of wks. in charge of P.W.D., Esseguibo dist., 9th Nov., 1895; student, Middle Temple, 1900; passed final bar exam., 1900; 2nd cls. offr. and clk. in charge of P.W.D., Esseguibo dist., 30th Mar., 1905; ch. clk., local govt. bd., 1st Dec., 1905; 1st cls. offr. and clk. in charge of P.W.D., Demerara dist., 5th Sept., 1906; sec. local govt. bd., and inspr. of dists., 22nd Sept., 1909; attached to L.G.B., London, 1911; ag. stip. mag., 24th Oct., 1912 to 17th Feb., 1914, and from 16th Mar. to 12th Oct., 1914; appld. by govt. to report on finances of town coun. of New Amsterdam, 1914; commr. to administer oaths to affidavits, 17th Apr., 1914; mem. bd. of agric., 1914; J.P. for the col., 1915; read, thanks of L.G.B. on two occasions for special work carried out for the bd.

SHANNON, C. M.—B. 1869; clerical asst., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1888; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1889; ditto, treas., June, 1890; 4th cls. clk., ditto, July, 1893; ag. acctn., mines dept., June, 1893 to Mar., 1894; govt. offr. (mining regna.) and J.P., Apr., 1897; 3rd grade land survr., lands and mines dept., Aug., 1902; 3rd cls. clk., ditto, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1908; ag. acctn., G.P.O., July to Sept., 1911; ag. paymaster of police, June, 1913 to Mar., 1914; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1913.

SHARPE, Sir Alfred, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. commr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy commr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as commr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; commr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; commr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.
SHARPE, H. B.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919.

SHARPE, Richard.—B. 1882; ed. Sexey's Schl., Somt. (Somt. county jnr. and inter. scholarships and hon. mention senr. scholarship); ent. Impl. cust. serv. by open compet. exam., and apptd. to London, 1903; London Univ. cert., polit. economy with sp. distina. in exchange, finance and taxation, 1907; seconded to govt. of Liberia as sub-inspr., cust., 1908-10; ch. inspr., cust. and finan. adviser, 1911-12; Br. receiver of Liberian cust., 1911-19; offl. recr., liquidating German property in Liberia, 1917-18; knight comdr., Order of African Redemption, 1913; treas., Dominica, Feb., 1920; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., collr. of cust. and excise, regir. of shipping, chmn., quarantine bd., Impl. trade corresp.; dep. contr., cust., Gold Coast, 3rd Jan., 1923.

SHAW, George Ernest, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prizeman in English prose; B.A., LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., 1900; collr. of land rev., Krian, 1901; mag., 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1913; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to War Office, directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919.

SHAW, Walter Sidney.—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908, to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admtd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911, to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stiltn s., 23rd May, 1921.

SHAWE, Henry Benjamin, C.M.G. (1924), I.S.O. (1917).—Clt. at Clan william, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. secy.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. o acctns., 14th Apl., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895 acctng. offr., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. CLK. AND ACOETING OFFR., 1ST MAR., 1901; ASST. UNDER COL. SEC., 1ST APR., 1902; AG. UNDER SEC. FOR AGRIC., SEPT., 1907, TO MAR., 1908; MEM. OF RHODESIAN PUB. SERV. BD. OF ENQUIRY, APR. TO SEPT., 1909; UNDER-SEC. FOR THE INTERIOR, UNION OF S. AFRICA, MAY, 1910; AG. SEC. FOR THE INTERIOR, 28TH SEPT., 1914; SEC. FOR THE INTERIOR, 1ST OCT., 1919.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., FERRYLAND (NEWFIELD), 1889-93; AGAIN IN 1897; MEM. EXEC. COUN. (WITHOUT PORTFOLIO), 1897; MEM. OF TREAS. BD., 1898; MAYOR OF ST. JOHN'S, 1902; MEM. EXEC. COUN., 1904.

SHEEDY, CAPT. FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., L.S.C., M.R.C.V.S.—ED. CLONGOWES, QUEEN'S COLL., CORK; LATE CAPT., E.A.P. FORCES; ENT. ARMY, A.V.C. 1911; VET. OFFR., E.A.P., JULY, 1914; SERVED THROUGHOUT GREAT WAR, 5TH AUG., 1914 TO 22ND SEPT., 1919 (E. AFRICA MOUNTED RIFLES AND E.A.V.C.); ADJUTANT, E.A.V.C., 9TH JAN., 1916; M.B.E. (MIL.), 1918, "1914-15" STAR, BRIT. WAR MED.; VICTORY MED.; DEP. CH. VET. OFFR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, 31ST OCT., 1919; AG. CH. VET. OFFR., MAY, 1920 TO MAR., 1921 AND APR., 1923 TO 1924.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ED. CHRISTIAN SCHLS., DUBLIN UNIV. AND UNIV. COLL., DUBLIN.—PTE., ARTISTE RIFLES, DEC., 1916; 2ND LIEUT., R.F.A., SEPT., 1917; DEMOB., MAY, 1919; CADET, F.M.S., MAR., 1920; AG. 2ND ASD. ADVISER, MUAR, MAR., 1921; AG. P.M.G., JOHORE, OCT., 1921; PASSED CADET, NOV., 1921; AG. COLL. LAND REV., JOHORE BHARU, JAN., 1922; SEC. RUBBER RESTRICTION, JOHORE, OCT., 1922; SUPERINT. OFFR., CLA. V., MAR., 1923.

SHELDON, HUGH FREDERICK, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (MANCH.)—ED. MANCHESTER GRAM. SCHL. AND OWEN'S COLL.; CIV. SURG., H.M. FORCES, AND CAPT. AND M.O., HEALTH BORDER POL., 1901-16; DIST. SURG. AND M.O.H., LAIRDBRAND, 1906-14; 37TH FIELD AMBULANCE, FRANCE, 1915-16; REGISTR., TEMP. MAJ., R.A.M.C., 1ST WESTERN GEN. HOSP., 1917; ASST. HEALTH OFFR., UNION OF S. AFRICA, 1920.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1890; ED. HARROW AND IN GERMANY; AG. VICE-CONSUL, GHENT, 1905; AG. VICE-CONSUL, HAVRE, 1906-7; AG. CONSUL, BORDEAUX, 1907; ASST. COLL., ZANZIBAR, 27TH DEC., 1907; SEC. TO FIRST MINISTER, 1909-10; COLL., (NOW DIST. COMMR.), PEMBA, 16TH OCT., 1912; AG. MAG., 30TH AUG., 1915; AG. CH. SEC., 5TH MAR. TO 17TH NOV., 1919; SEC. COMMR., ZANZIBAR, 20TH SEPT., 1923; 4TH CLS., ORDER OF THE ALIYEH AND 3RD CLS., ORDER OF THE BRILLIANT STAR OF ZANZIBAR.
SHELTON, C. F., M.R.C.S. (ENG.), L.R.C.P. (LOND.), M.D. (LOND.), B.S.—CIVIL SURGEON, SERBIAN RED CROSS, 1913; SURGEON LIEUT., 1914-19; MED. OFFR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, SEPT., 1921.
SHELVER, E. A.—PREVENTIVE OFFR., CUSTOMS DEPT., KENYA, JULY, 1919; OUST. ASST., NOV., 1922.

SHEPHERD, FREDERICK REECE.—B. 1869; SUPT. OF SKERRETT'S TRAINING SCHL., ANTIGUA, LEEWARD IS., 1891-1905; CURATOR AND AGRIC. SUPT., ST. KITTS, NEVIS, FEB., 1904; AG. SUPT., SUGAR CANE EXPERIMENTS, ANTIGUA, 1899-1904; DELEG. TO VARIOUS AGRIC. CONFERS. HEUD BY IMPL. COMMR.; AG. SUPT. OF AGRIC., LEEWARD IS., JULY TO NOV., 1910.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B.CH. (CANTAB.).—B. 1871; CIVIL SURG. IN CHARGE OF H.M.'S TROOPS, N.E. DIST., ENG., MAY, 1888, TO MAY, 1899; SPEC. PLAGUE MED. OFFR., INDIA, MAY, 1899, TO NOV., 1900; HOUSE SURG., GEN. HOSP., SINGAPORE, NOV. 1900, 1901; SUPER. COL. SURG., S. STILTM., 1ST MAR., 1901; SPEC. MISSION TO BRUNEI, AUG., 1904; COL. SURG., PROV. WELLSEELEY, AUG., 1906; SENIOR MED. OFFR., SINGAPORE, JULY, 1911.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; APPTD. AFTER COMPET. EXAM., ASST. CLK., C.O., APR., 1918; ON MILY. SERV., 1918 TO 1920; CLER. OFFR., 1ST JAN., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—ED. AT CASTLEKNOCK COLL. AND TRIN. COLL., DUBLIN; CALLED TO THE BAR, IRELAND, 1907, WENT CONNAUGHT CIRCUIT; JUDEL. CLK., NYASALAND PROT., JULY, 1908; AG. ATTY.-GEN., NOV., 1909, TO JAN., 1910; AG. JUDGE OF HIGH CT., DEC., 1909, TO JAN., 1910; AG. ATTY.-GEN., JULY, 1911, TO MAR., 1912; APPTD. ASST. ATTY.-GEN., APR., 1912; AG. ATTY.-GEN., AUG., 1912; TOWN MAG., E.A.P., 1913; AG. PUISNE JUDGE, JULY, 1919; 3RD PUISNE JUDGE, KENYA, APR., 1920.

SHERWOOD, MONTAGUE EARLE, M.B.E.—B. 1884; CADET, S. STILTM., 21ST OCT., 1907; ATTACHED TO COL. SEC.'S OFFICE, 1ST JAN., 1908; ATTACHED TO LAND OFFICE, SINGAPORE, 23RD JAN., 1908; DEP. COLL. OF LAND REV. AND DEP. REGISTR. OF DEEDS, SINGAPORE, 31ST JAN., 1908; AG. DIST. OFFR., LABUAN, 29TH JAN. TO 16TH NOV., 1909; ATTACHED TO DIST. OFFICE, ALOR GAJAH, 28TH DEC., 1910; PASSED FINAL EXAM. IN MALAY, JAN., 1911; PASSED CADET, 26TH JAN., 1911; ATTACHED TO LAND OFFICE, MALACCA, 1ST MAR., 1911; AG. DIST. OFFR., JASIN, 7TH APR., 1911; OFFR. OF CLA. V., 13TH MAY, 1911; AG. 3RD MAG., PENANG, 15TH OCT., 1913; AG. DIST. OFFR., ALOR GAJAH, 18TH FEB., 1914; AG. ASST. TO RES., MALACCA, 1ST SEPT., 1915; AG. HARBR.-MAST., MALACCA, IN ADDITION TO OTHER DUTIES, 26TH JAN., 1916; PRIV. SEC. AND A.D.C. TO GOV., 1ST AUG., 1916; OFFR. OF CLA. III., 16TH NOV., 1919; AG. PRIV. SEC. TO H.E. THE GOV., FEB., 1920; AG. SEC. TO HIGH COMMRS., MARCH, 1921.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. 1896; ON MILY. SERVICE (FRANCE) 1914-18; ON STAFF, 1918-19; PRIV. SECY. TO GOV., AND CLK. TO COUNS., SEYCHELLES, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—LABOUR INSPECTION OFFR., E. AFRICA PROT., APR., 1919.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—B. 1879; APPTD., AFTER COMPET. EXAM., 2ND DIV. CLK., AND ASSIGNED TO C.O., DEC., 1898; MINOR STAFF CLK., 1911; REGISTR. OF COL. LAWS, 1ST APR., 1922.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, M.B., CH.B.—MED. OFFR., NYASALAND, 1909; TRANSFD. TO UGANDA, 1909; RE-TRANSFD. AS MED. OFFR., NYASALAND, 1910; MED. OFFR., E.A.F., 1912; DEP. PRIM. MED. OFFR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, 15TH OCT., 1919.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1869; ED. AT WALKERTON HIGH SCHL. AND QUEEN'S UNIV., CANADA (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1886) AND GLASGOW AND EDIN. UNIVS.; ASST. PROF. OF PHILOSOPHY, QUEEN'S UNIV., 1886; LECT. AND PROF. OF POLIT. SCIENCE, 1889-1908; CIV. SER. COMMR., CANADA, 1908-1917; CHMN. OF HISTORICAL MANUSCRIPTS PUBLICATION BOARD, OCT., 1917; WRITER ON HISTORY, BANKING AND ECONOMICS; DEL. TO INTERNAT. CONGRESS OF HISTORY OF AMERICA, RIO DE JANEIRO, 1922.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ED. ETON AND KING'S COLL., CAMBRIDGE; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; APPTD. JNR. CLK., STORE DEPT., I.O., OCT., 1900; TRANSFD. TO JUDL. AND PUB. DEPT., MAR., 1901; JOINT EDITOR, "INDIA OFFICE LIST," OCT., 1901; PRIV. SECY. TO PERMUT. UNDER-SEC. OF STATE, JULY, 1902; SENR. CLK., POLIT. DEPT., MAR., 1906; ASST. SEC., POLIT. DEPT., OCT., 1912; SEC., POLIT. DEPT., MAR., 1917; AG. ASST. UNDER SEC. OF STATE C.O., 1ST MAR., 1921.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ED. KING'S SCHL., CANTERBURY, AND GONVILLE AND CAIUS COLL., CAMBRIDGE (STANHOPE EXHIBR., OPEN CLASS EXHIBR., 1911, SCHOL., 1912); 1ST CLK., THEOLOG. TRIPOS, PT. I., 1914; 2ND
SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1918.
SIMSON, COLIN COAPE—Chief med. offr., Papua, 19th Oct., 1908.

SIMSON, ERIC ANDREW.—B. 1895; ed. Wellington Coll. (schlr., 1909), and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mily. serv., R.F.A., 17th div., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1916; staff, 12th div., Feb. to Dec., 1916; capt., R.A.F., Jan., 1917 to June, 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 3rd Aug., 1920.
SIMSON, J. A.—Ed. in England, Germany and Switzerland; agt.-gen. in India for the British Protectorates in Africa.
SINCLAIR, SIR ARCHIBALD, BART., C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1890; major, 2nd Life Guards; personal mily. seoy., to Mr. Churchill.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine coman. which visited Europe in 1909; unsuc., cand., legis., ass., P.E.I., 1908; sl. to H.C., g.e., 1917; ro-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admn., 29th Dec., 1921.

SINCLAIR, JOHN HOUSTON, C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1871; apptd. to col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Dec., 1893; local auditor, E.A.P. and Uganda Rlwy., Dec., 1895; H.M. vice-consul, Zanzibar, Apr., 1899; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1903, to June, 1904, Feb. to May, 1905; consul, 1st July, 1906; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Aug., 1906, to Feb., 1907, Mar. to Oct., 1911, and Feb., 1913 to Apr., 1914; ch., sec., Zanzibar Prot., 1st Jan., 1914; pres. of internat. bureau at Zanzibar for the suppression of the slave trade, 1914 and 1916; ag. res., Aug., 1916, to Apr., 1917, and from Mar. to Dec., 1919; also ag. high commr., 8th Nov. to 2nd Dec., 1919; ag. high commr. and res., 8th Aug. to 1st Oct., 1920; ag. res., 1st Oct., 1921; res., 10th Jan., 1922.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comn., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBECK, M.A., F.L.C.—Senr. chem., dept. of agr., Pretoria, 1911; senr. chem., govt. chem. lab., Grahamstown, 1912; govt. analyst, Cape Town, 1913; transfd., dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923.

SINGAPORE, LORD BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. CHARLES JAMES FERGUSON-DAVIE, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of St. Paul, Preston, 1896-99; dom. chap. to Bishop of Lahore, 1899-1902; S.P.G. mission at Rawari, 1902-1907; S.P.G. mission at Rawal Pindi, 1907-09; consecrated Lord Bishop of Singapore, 24th Aug., 1909.

SIRCOM, HAROLD SEBASTIAN.—B. 1878; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, May, 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Nov., 1907; cls. V., Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; seconded, commr. of cust., Johore, 1913; cls. IV., Nov., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, Dec., 1917; cls. III., Jan., 1918; seconded, asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, June, 1918; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. commr. of lands and mines, Johore, June, 1920; asst. adviser, Muar, Apr., 1921; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1922; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1923; chmn., sany., Apr., 1923; sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1923; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addition, Oct., 1923.

SISNETT, HERBERT KORTRIGHT McDONNELL.—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A.
